metadata.description: Azure region used for landing-zone metadata and subscription-scope deployments.
Short landing-zone identifier used in names, tags, and budget resources.
Environment stamp for the landing zone.
Owning team or platform contact for the shared landing zone.
Cost center, cost code, or internal chargeback reference.
Additional tags merged into the shared governance baseline.
Existing Log Analytics workspace resource ID used for diagnostic settings defaults. Leave empty until observability resources exist.
Monthly subscription budget amount in the subscription billing currency.
Budget period start date in ISO 8601 format, for example 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z.
Email recipients for budget threshold notifications.
Hub platform network configuration, including address planning and DNS baseline metadata.

breakGlass.spokeReachabilityRequirement: Spokes that require operator access must peer to the hub with allowGatewayTransit and useRemoteGateways enabled.

metadata.description: Operator connectivity configuration for the Point-to-Site VPN path.
Shared platform service configuration for the hub-hosted central registry and secret store.
Runner execution platform configuration for the shared Container Apps environment and GitHub runner job.

Optional workload spoke definitions. Each spoke must use non-overlapping prefixes carved from hubNetworkConfig.futureSpokeSupernet and reserve room for workload, private endpoint, and future growth subnets.
Azure region used for naming conventions and governance metadata.
Short landing-zone identifier used in names and tags.
Environment stamp for the landing zone.
Owning team or platform contact.
Cost center, cost code, or internal chargeback reference.
Additional tags merged into the shared governance baseline.
Existing Log Analytics workspace resource ID used for diagnostics defaults.
Monthly subscription budget amount.
Budget period start date in ISO 8601 format.
Email recipients for budget threshold notifications.

diagnosticsDefaults.notes: Apply this baseline to supported platform resources when their modules are introduced.

registry.premiumJustification: Private Link requires Premium and is the accepted cost exception for the central shared registry.

exceptionApprovalRequiredFor: Azure Container Apps dedicated workload profiles
reviewNotes: Keep shared platform services in the hub and onboard workload services to spokes later.
Prefer consumption-based or entry-level SKUs until production workload evidence justifies higher spend.
Use Azure Container Registry Premium for the single shared registry because Private Link is part of the baseline private connectivity posture.
Enable diagnostics only after routing to an existing workspace to avoid orphaned ingestion cost.

metadata.description: Name of the subscription budget resource.
Monthly budget amount in the subscription billing currency.
Budget period start date in ISO 8601 format.
Email recipients for budget notifications.

subscriptionScope.managementGroupAlignment: Attach the subscription to the platform landing-zone management group before onboarding workloads.

metadata.description: Azure region for the hub network foundation.
Tags applied to the hub network resources.
Name of the hub virtual network.
Subnet names used for the hub platform network.
Hub platform network configuration, including address ranges, subnet prefixes, and DNS baseline metadata.
Address pool assigned to Point-to-Site VPN operators.

properties.description: Allows break-glass operators from the P2S pool to reach hub shared services.
Keeps shared services private by default.
Allows break-glass operators to reach private endpoints for diagnostics.
Prevents direct internet reachability to private endpoints.
Allows break-glass operators to reach internal HTTPS endpoints exposed through the Container Apps environment.
Keeps the runner subnet private by default.

notes: The hub is reserved for platform connectivity, shared services, and runner infrastructure.
The Container Apps infrastructure subnet is pre-sized to avoid later subnet replacement.
The future spoke supernet is intentionally separate from the hub VNet to keep peering straightforward.
Use Azure-provided DNS initially and link hub-hosted private DNS zones to the hub and approved spokes.
Keep DNS central in the hub so future spoke onboarding does not require per-spoke resolvers.

metadata.description: Azure region for the workload spoke network.
Tags applied to the workload spoke resources.
Friendly workload name for the spoke.
Normalized token used in resource names for the spoke.
Name of the workload spoke virtual network.
Address prefixes assigned to the workload spoke VNet.
Subnet prefixes used inside the workload spoke.
Hub VNet address prefixes used to deny non-approved platform access.
Hub shared-services subnet prefix that workloads are allowed to reach through the hub peering.
Hub private-endpoints subnet prefix that workloads are allowed to reach through the hub peering.
Future spoke supernet reserved by the landing zone for all workload spokes.
Point-to-Site VPN client pool used for break-glass operator reachability.

properties.description: Allows traffic that stays inside the workload spoke boundary.
Allows approved break-glass operators to reach the workload subnet through the hub gateway.
Prevents hub platform and runner subnets from initiating traffic into the workload subnet.
Blocks lateral traffic from other spokes that share the reserved spoke supernet.
Keeps the workload subnet private by default.
Allows traffic that stays inside the workload spoke boundary.
Allows workloads to reach approved shared services hosted in the hub.
Allows workloads to consume hub-hosted private endpoints such as the shared registry path.
Prevents workloads from reaching non-approved hub subnets over the peering.
Blocks lateral traffic from the workload subnet to other spokes.
Allows workload resources inside the same spoke to consume local private endpoints.
Allows approved break-glass operators to reach spoke private endpoints for diagnostics.
Prevents hub platform and runner subnets from initiating traffic into the private-endpoints subnet.
Blocks lateral traffic from other spokes that share the reserved spoke supernet.
Keeps the spoke private-endpoints subnet private by default.
Allows the private-endpoints subnet to return traffic only inside the local spoke boundary.
Prevents private endpoints from becoming a pivot into the wider hub platform network.
Blocks lateral traffic from the private-endpoints subnet to other spokes.

constraints: [format('Allocate workload spoke prefixes from the reserved future spoke supernet {0}.', parameters('futureSpokeSupernet'))]
[format('Do not overlap workload spokes with hub prefixes {0} or with other spokes.', join(parameters('hubAddressPrefixes'), ', '))]
[format('Reserve the declared future prefix {0} for later in-spoke growth so workloads do not need renumbering.', parameters('subnetPrefixes').reservedFuture)]

allowedInbound: [format('Break-glass operators from {0} may reach workload and private-endpoint subnets.', parameters('vpnClientAddressPool'))]
Traffic that stays inside the local spoke address space remains allowed.
allowedOutbound: [format('Workloads can reach the hub shared-services subnet {0}.', parameters('hubSharedServicesPrefix'))]
[format('Workloads can reach the hub private-endpoints subnet {0}.', parameters('hubPrivateEndpointsPrefix'))]
Private-endpoint resources return traffic only inside the same spoke.
blockedByDefault: Hub platform subnets other than approved shared-services and private-endpoints prefixes are denied.
[format('Spoke-to-spoke traffic across the reserved supernet {0} is denied at the subnet boundary.', parameters('futureSpokeSupernet'))]
Internet-originated inbound traffic remains denied by default.

metadata.description: Name of the local VNet where the peering is created.
Name of the peering resource on the local VNet.
Remote VNet resource ID.
Whether virtual network access is enabled across the peering.
Whether forwarded traffic is allowed across the peering.
Whether the local VNet shares its gateway with the remote VNet.
Whether the local VNet uses the remote gateway.
Name of the local VNet where the peering is created.
Name of the peering resource on the local VNet.
Remote VNet resource ID.
Whether virtual network access is enabled across the peering.
Whether forwarded traffic is allowed across the peering.
Whether the local VNet shares its gateway with the remote VNet.
Whether the local VNet uses the remote gateway.
Azure region for the operator connectivity resources.
Tags applied to operator connectivity resources.
Name of the public IP used by the VPN gateway.
Name of the Point-to-Site virtual network gateway.
Gateway subnet resource ID from the hub VNet.
Low-cost VPN gateway SKU for Point-to-Site access.
Address pool assigned to Point-to-Site VPN clients.
Microsoft Entra tenant ID used for Point-to-Site VPN authentication.
Microsoft-registered Azure VPN Client audience ID.
Break-glass operating model metadata emitted as deployment outputs.

reachabilityNotes: P2S VPN is the primary low-cost operator access path into the platform hub.
Operators connect with Azure VPN Client and receive an address from the dedicated VPN client pool.
Spokes that need break-glass access must consume the hub gateway through VNet peering with gateway transit enabled.

operatingModel: Use just-in-time group membership for day-to-day operators and keep emergency accounts separate from routine administration.
Test VPN profile download and emergency access at least quarterly.
Limit the break-glass path to hub platform resources and approved spokes; keep workloads outside the hub.

metadata.description: Azure region for shared platform services.
Tags applied to shared platform services.
Hub VNet resource ID linked to private DNS zones.
Subnet resource ID used for private endpoints.
Name of the central Azure Container Registry.
Name of the shared platform Key Vault that stores runner secrets and future platform secrets.
Shared-service hosting configuration for central registry and secret storage.

assumptions: Hub-hosted shared services stay in the platform resource group and are consumed from spokes through private endpoints and hub-linked private DNS zones.
Future shared platform services that need private consumption should reuse the shared services resource group and the private endpoints subnet instead of introducing workload resources into the hub.

containerRegistry.name: [parameters('containerRegistryName')]
imageConsumptionPattern: Runner jobs pull images from the central registry with a dedicated managed identity.
Future workload spokes consume shared images over the hub-linked privatelink.azurecr.io zone.
Enable ACR ARM-audience authentication before first runner image pull so Container Apps managed identities can authenticate without admin credentials.

secretVault.name: [parameters('keyVaultName')]
secretHandlingNotes: Store GitHub personal access tokens or equivalent bootstrap secrets in Key Vault rather than as deployment-time plain text parameters.
Grant the runner execution identity least-privilege Key Vault secret access and keep operator access on separate RBAC assignments.

metadata.description: Azure region for the runner execution platform.
Tags applied to runner platform resources.
Name of the Container Apps managed environment hosting runner jobs.
Name of the GitHub Actions runner job definition.
Name of the runner execution identity used inside the job.
Name of the runner registry identity used for ACR pulls.
Name of the central Azure Container Registry in this resource group.
Name of the shared platform Key Vault in this resource group.
Subnet resource ID delegated to the Container Apps environment.
Runner execution configuration for the Container Apps environment and GitHub runner job.

environment.name: [parameters('containerAppsEnvironmentName')]
boundaryNotes: The Container Apps environment is the runner execution boundary and is isolated from shared services by subnet separation.
The environment is internal-only and uses the dedicated hub runner subnet rather than sharing workload spokes or operator connectivity resources.

execution.name: [parameters('runnerExecutionIdentityName')]
execution.workloadAccessModel: Assign Azure RBAC for deployment targets to this identity outside the shared platform baseline.

job.name: [parameters('runnerJobName')]
privateConnectivity: Runner executions start inside the hub-integrated Container Apps environment and resolve shared services through hub-linked private DNS zones.
Image pulls use the dedicated registry identity against the private central ACR, and secret retrieval uses Key Vault over private endpoint connectivity.
secretHandlingModel: The job references a Key Vault secret URI for the GitHub PAT instead of storing PAT values in the template or repository.
Only the runner execution identity receives Key Vault secret access; operators and future workload identities remain separate.
platformConstraints: Use this runner platform only for private repositories.
Container Apps jobs do not support Docker-in-Docker workflows.

standard.reservedFuturePrefix: Provide subnetPrefixes.reservedFuture in each workloadSpokes entry to keep room for later in-spoke growth.
isolationNotes: Workloads stay in separate VNets carved from the reserved spoke supernet and never share the hub VNet.
Subnet NSGs allow hub access only to the shared-services and private-endpoints prefixes and deny traffic to the wider hub and to other spokes.
Break-glass operator reachability comes through the hub VPN gateway and does not require direct spoke-to-spoke trust.
